content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


March 8, 1989


Lucille Bourbeau
925 Sonoma Avenue
Modesto, CA 95355

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-89-127

Dear Ms. Bourbeau:

You have written requesting information regarding disclosure
requirements for candidates for the Grand Jury. Since you have
not requested advice on your behalf or at the request of an
official with duties under the Political Reform Act (the "Act"),1/
we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2/

Under the provisions of the Act, local agencies are required
to promulgate conflict of interest codes. (Section 87300.) The
conflict of interest codes must enumerate the positions within the
agency which may materially affect financial interests. Financial
disclosure requirements for said positions must also be
enumerated. (See Section 87300(a) and Regulation 18730, copies
enclosed.)

Grand juries are local government agencies and are therefore
subject to the disclosure requirements of the Act. (See
Whittington Advice Letter, No. A-85-185, copy enclosed.)


___________________________
1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Lucille Bourbeau
March 8, 1989
Page 2

     Please do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901 if you
have further questions.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Bianca M. Breeze
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:BMB:ld

Enclosure

FPPc

FEB 1 8 48 AM '09

925 Sonoma Avenue
Modesto, California 95355
January 29, 1989

Mr. John H. Larson, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street #900
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sir:

Late last year a friend of mine was a candidate for the 1989
Grand Jury of Stanislaus County. This individual was quite
outraged about forms which required disclosure of finances
and business transactions.

This person asked me to inquire about the matter. I wrote a
letter to our Board of Supervisors. It took a while and then
I had a call from our County Attorney. I could accept his
explanation except for one thing. That was that this type
of inquiring was started at the behest of the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

I cannot see the connection. The Grand Jury should not be
political, the powers that be would insist that it is not,
so where do you fit in and why?

My friend refused to complete the process. Had I been a
candidate, I also would have refused.

Sincerely,

Lucille Bourbeau
Lucille Bourbeau

State of California

Memorandum

To : Diane Griffiths                                Date : February 6, 1989


From : John H. Larson
        FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

Subject : Letter received from Lucille Bourbeau

        Attached hereto is a letter from Lucille Bourbeau.  Could you
please prepare a response.

Attachment

89-154

FPPC

FEB | 8 49 AH '09

925 Sonoma Avenue
Modesto, California 95355
January 29, 1989

Mr. John H. Larson, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street #800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sir:

Late last year a friend of mine was a candidate for the 1989
Grand Jury of Stanislaus County. This individual was quite
outraged about forms which required disclosure of finances
and business transactions.

This person asked me to inquire about the matter. I wrote a
letter to our Board of Supervisors. It took a while and then
I had a call from our County Attorney. I could accept his
explanation except for one thing. That was that this type
of inquiring was started at the behest of the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

I cannot see the connection. The Grand Jury should not be
political, the powers that be would insist that it is not,
so where do you fit in and why?

My friend refused to complete the process. Had I been a
candidate, I also would have refused.

Sincerely,

Lucille Bourbeau

Lucille Bourbeau

sections.question: You have written requesting information regarding disclosure requirements for candidates for the Grand Jury.
sections.conclusion: Grand juries are local government agencies and are therefore subject to the disclosure requirements of the Act. Local agencies are required to promulgate conflict of interest codes that enumerate positions which may materially affect financial interests and specify financial disclosure requirements for those positions.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the disclosure requirements for candidates for the Grand Jury under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Grand Juries are local government agencies subject to the Political Reform Act's disclosure requirements, including conflict of interest codes that specify which positions require financial disclosures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (dated February 6, 1989) and FPPC response letter (dated March 8, 1989). Response is informal advice, not formal advice letter. Incoming request expresses concern about political implications of disclosure requirements for Grand Jury candidates.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have written requesting information regarding disclosure requirements for candidates for the Grand Jury.

CONCLUSION: Grand juries are local government agencies and are therefore subject to the disclosure requirements of the Act. Local agencies are required to promulgate conflict of interest codes that enumerate positions which may materially affect financial interests and specify financial disclosure requirements for those positions.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel Diane Griffiths responds to Lucille Bourbeau's informal request for information about disclosure requirements for Grand Jury candidates, explaining that Grand Juries are local agencies subject to the Act's conflict of interest code and financial disclosure provisions.
